page.title: Best Sunset Times for Your Marbella Yacht Charter

data.meta_description: Plan your marbella yacht charter sunset time with exact 2025/2026 schedules. Best hours, routes & boats for sunset cruises from Puerto Banús.
data.summary: This guide provides precise sunset times for Marbella yacht charters, including seasonal tables, recommended departure windows, and the best boats and routes from Puerto Banús for an unforgettable evening on the water.
key_facts: Sunset times in Marbella range from 18:00 (Dec) to 21:45 (Jun).
Best departure window: 90 minutes before official sunset.
Top sunset routes: Marbella coastline, Cabopino, or Estepona.
Popular sunset boats: Astondoa 40 (€749/2h), Azimut 39, Mangusta 80.
All charters include skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks & IVA.
Puerto Banús is the main departure point for our fleet.

faq.q: What is the best time for a sunset yacht charter in Marbella?
faq.a: The ideal time is 90 minutes before official sunset. This gives you time to cruise out, anchor, watch the sun dip below the horizon, and enjoy golden hour light. For a summer sunset at 21:30, depart around 20:00. In winter, aim for 16:30 departure for a 18:00 sunset.

faq.q: Do sunset charters include drinks and snacks?
faq.a: Yes, every charter includes water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, and light snacks. Our licensed skipper handles navigation so you can relax. The price also covers fuel, insurance, and Spanish VAT (21%).

faq.q: Which boat is best for a sunset cruise with a group?
faq.a: For groups up to 9, the Astondoa 40 (€749/2h) or Azimut 39 are excellent. For up to 12 guests, the Mangusta 80 (from €4,719/4h) offers luxury and space. All depart from Puerto Banús.

faq.q: Can I swim during a sunset charter?
faq.a: Yes, most sunset charters include a stop for swimming. Popular spots are Cala del Faro, Cala Cortés, or near Cabopino. The skipper will anchor in calm, sheltered bays. Bring a towel and swimwear.

faq.q: What happens if the weather is bad on my sunset charter?
faq.a: Your safety is priority. If conditions are unsafe, we reschedule or offer a full refund. Our skippers monitor forecasts closely. Summer evenings are usually calm, but we recommend booking flexible dates.

faq.q: How do I book a sunset yacht charter in Marbella?
faq.a: Contact us via WhatsApp or our website. Provide your preferred date, group size, and boat preference. We'll confirm availability and send a booking link. For Tier B boats like the Mangusta 80, pricing is on request.

faq.q: Is the sunset view better from the sea or land?
faq.a: From the sea, you get an unobstructed 360° horizon. The Sierra Blanca mountains and La Concha peak frame the sun setting behind the coast. It's far more dramatic than from a beach or restaurant.

faq.q: What is the best route for a sunset charter from Puerto Banús?
faq.a: Two popular routes: head east toward Marbella Marina and the Golden Mile for city lights, or west toward Cabopino and Cala del Faro for a wilder coastline. Both offer stunning sunset views.

data.body_html:
Planning your marbella yacht charter sunset time is the key to an unforgettable evening on the Costa del Sol. Whether you're celebrating a special occasion or simply soaking up the Mediterranean magic, knowing exactly when to depart and which route to take makes all the difference. This guide gives you precise sunset schedules for 2025/2026, recommended departure windows, and the best boats from Puerto Banús.

Why Sunset Is the Best Time for a Yacht Charter

The golden hour transforms Marbella's coastline. As the sun sinks behind La Concha mountain, the sky turns shades of orange, pink, and purple. The sea calms, the temperature drops, and the city lights begin to twinkle. From the deck of a private yacht, you get an unobstructed view that no beach or restaurant can match. Plus, sunset charters are typically shorter (2–4 hours), making them perfect for an evening out without a full-day commitment.

Marbella Sunset Times 2025/2026

Sunset times vary by season. Here's a quick reference for Puerto Banús (all times local):

MonthApprox SunsetRecommended Departure
January18:2016:50
February18:5017:20
March19:2017:50
April20:0018:30
May20:4519:15
June21:3020:00
July21:4520:15
August21:1519:45
September20:1518:45
October19:1517:45
November18:1516:45
December18:0016:30

These are approximate; exact times shift daily. Always check the forecast and confirm with your skipper.

Best Boats for a Sunset Charter

Our fleet has options for every group size and budget. For a quick 2-hour sunset cruise, the Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 pax) or Azimut 39 (12.5m, 11 pax) are perfect. Both are priced at €749 for 2 hours, including skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, and VAT. For a longer evening, the 8-hour option is €2,299. If you want extra luxury, the Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax) starts at €4,719 for 4 hours and includes a free jet ski. All depart from Puerto Banús. Browse our full fleet on the [Boat Rental Marbella](/) page.

Top Sunset Routes from Puerto Banús

Two routes stand out for sunset charters:

  • East to Marbella Marina & Golden Mile: Cruise past the luxury hotels, beach clubs, and the iconic Marbella skyline. As the sun sets, the city lights reflect on the water. This is the classic Marbella sunset experience.
  • West to Cabopino & Cala del Faro: Head toward the quieter coastline of Cabopino, with its pine-fringed dunes and the secluded Cala del Faro. This route offers a more natural, wild sunset with fewer boats around.

Your skipper will choose the best spot based on wind and sea conditions. Both routes take about 15–20 minutes from Puerto Banús to reach prime sunset anchorages.

What's Included in Your Sunset Charter

Every sunset charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, insurance, and safety equipment. You'll also enjoy unlimited water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, and light snacks. The price includes Spanish IVA (21%). No hidden extras. Just arrive at Puerto Banús, step aboard, and relax. For sunset trips, we recommend bringing a light jacket or sweater — even in summer, the sea breeze can be cool after sunset.

How to Book Your Sunset Charter

Booking is simple. Contact us via WhatsApp or through our website. Tell us your preferred date, group size, and any special requests. For Tier A boats (Astondoa 40, Azimut 39), the price is fixed at €749 for 2 hours. For Tier B boats like the Mangusta 80, pricing is on request. We'll confirm availability and send a booking link. For more details, check our [blog](/blog/) for seasonal tips and destination guides.

Frequently Asked Questions

What is the best time for a sunset yacht charter in Marbella?

The ideal time is 90 minutes before official sunset. This gives you time to cruise out, anchor, watch the sun dip below the horizon, and enjoy golden hour light. For a summer sunset at 21:30, depart around 20:00. In winter, aim for 16:30 departure for a 18:00 sunset.

Do sunset charters include drinks and snacks?

Yes, every charter includes water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, and light snacks. Our licensed skipper handles navigation so you can relax. The price also covers fuel, insurance, and Spanish VAT (21%).

Which boat is best for a sunset cruise with a group?

For groups up to 9, the Astondoa 40 (€749/2h) or Azimut 39 are excellent. For up to 12 guests, the Mangusta 80 (from €4,719/4h) offers luxury and space. All depart from Puerto Banús.

Can I swim during a sunset charter?

Yes, most sunset charters include a stop for swimming. Popular spots are Cala del Faro, Cala Cortés, or near Cabopino. The skipper will anchor in calm, sheltered bays. Bring a towel and swimwear.

What happens if the weather is bad on my sunset charter?

Your safety is priority. If conditions are unsafe, we reschedule or offer a full refund. Our skippers monitor forecasts closely. Summer evenings are usually calm, but we recommend booking flexible dates.

How do I book a sunset yacht charter in Marbella?

Contact us via WhatsApp or our website. Provide your preferred date, group size, and boat preference. We'll confirm availability and send a booking link. For Tier B boats like the Mangusta 80, pricing is on request.

Is the sunset view better from the sea or land?

From the sea, you get an unobstructed 360° horizon. The Sierra Blanca mountains and La Concha peak frame the sun setting behind the coast. It's far more dramatic than from a beach or restaurant.

What is the best route for a sunset charter from Puerto Banús?

Two popular routes: head east toward Marbella Marina and the Golden Mile for city lights, or west toward Cabopino and Cala del Faro for a wilder coastline. Both offer stunning sunset views.
